criteria.label: Compute IQR fences by hand and state which values are flagged.
criteria.example: Given Q1 = 18, Q3 = 42, show IQR = 24, lower fence = −18, upper fence = 78, and flag any value outside [−18, 78].

criteria.label: Implement IQR or z-score detection in pandas/NumPy on a sample column.
criteria.example: Write five lines that compute Q1, Q3, IQR, and a boolean `is_outlier` Series for `df['amount']`.

criteria.label: Clip a feature using bounds fit on training data and applied to test data.
criteria.example: Fit `lo, hi = train['price'].quantile([0.01, 0.99])`, clip train and test with the same lo/hi, and report max before/after.

criteria.label: Explain when to remove, clip, or use a robust model for the same flagged point.
criteria.example: For a $4M salary typo in HR data vs a legitimate $4M executive compensation in a mixed C-suite dataset, justify remove vs clip vs Huber regression in two sentences each.

criteria.label: Contrast IQR and z-score detection on skewed versus near-normal data.
criteria.example: Sketch a right-skewed income histogram and argue which method flags the tail more sensibly and why mean-based z-scores can under-flag billionaires.
